arguments.task: Open the official iPayX documentation site at https://ipayx.ai/docs and inspect the page content. Determine whether it is live, readable, and whether it documents API authentication, API reference details, pricing/access gating, or developer setup. If the docs page is thin or JS-rendered, navigate within the official iPayX domain to any linked developer, API, authentication, pricing, signup, contact, or product pages that are first-party and relevant. Also inspect the main website for pricing, contact-sales, or developer access language. Return exact page URLs visited and concise excerpts that support: what iPayX is, auth methods, whether API access is self-serve or gated, API surface type, and any rate limit notes. Do not use third-party sites.

Inspect the official iPayX website for product description, developer/API links, pricing or access pages, and any indication of self-serve signup versus contact-sales/admin approval. Open any first-party docs, API, auth, pricing, or contact pages you find. Return exact URLs visited and excerpts supporting structured research fields only.

raw_result.description: iPayX is an FX audit platform that exposes forensic foreign-exchange analysis tools and AI-agent integrations for checking rates, auditing transactions, and generating reports.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official developers page documents Bearer-token authentication using a production API key in the Authorization header (example: 'Authorization: Bearer ipx_live_your_key'). The MCP page also indicates some public tools can be used without an API key and are instead IP rate-limited, but the documented authenticated API method is a bearer token.
raw_result.gating_reason: The pricing page lists a free tier ($0, 5 audits lifetime) and paid self-serve plans, and the site exposes a direct sign-up flow. Contact sales is mentioned for institutions, but standard developer access is self-serve.

api_surface.breadth: Official first-party pages document a JSON REST audit API with bearer-token auth and named operations such as check_fx_rate, audit_transaction, and full_forensic_report, plus a native MCP endpoint for AI agents at mcp.ipayx.ai.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The official MCP page documents an MCP server endpoint at https://mcp.ipayx.ai/mcp for agent clients such as Claude, Cursor, Cline, and Perplexity.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The MCP page states public tools are IP rate-limited. Developer materials also note HTTP 429 responses when limits are exceeded and reference tier-based usage limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: The provided hint URL https://ipayx.ai/docs returned a 404, but first-party developer information is available on the live /developers, /mcp, /pricing, and homepage URLs. The product explicitly markets MCP-native agent integration alongside REST-style API access.
